Merge branch 'var-1--rework-render-architecture'
human: instructions for refactoring

Refactor rendering to use screen-space coordinates for improved scaling and visual consistency. Implemented a "screen-space rendering" architecture where polygons, labels, axes, and hover highlights are drawn based on screen coordinates rather than world coordinates. This ensures that visual elements like stroke widths, hover radii, and dash patterns remain consistent and legible regardless of the zoom level. Added helper functions worldToScreen, getStrokeWidthPx, getStrokeWidthWorld, and getPolygonScreenPoints to facilitate this transformation and updated drawing logic across the Tile Editor to utilize these new helpers. Also adjusted hit testing and hover detection to align with the new coordinate system.
Merge branch 'center-view'
Improve timeline group label readability by adding semi-transparent badges with padding, darker background, and borders behind the text to prevent overlap with timeline lines.
Added a Timeline‑only “Center View” button in the center‑view worktree. It resets zoom/pan via resetTimelineView() and redraws, and it’s hidden unless Display Mode = Timeline.

Move the timeline scale ticks to the bottom of the scale area, closer to the timeline content, to improve visual connection.

Add milestone labels for larger time units (years, months, days, etc.) to the timeline scale. This ensures that major boundaries remain visible even when zoomed in to smaller units like hours or minutes, prioritizing their labels over the smaller unit ticks.
Fix timeline scale label overlap by ensuring only the larger unit label is drawn when ticks coincide

feat(timeline): replace quarterHour with tenMinute scale unit and fix unit ordering
Fixed the unit ordering and added a warning comment so it doesn’t get changed accidentally. Also updated the selection logic to match the smallest→largest ordering.

Simplified the scale selection logic back to the original approach (now with quarterHour inserted) and removed the range-length filter (bug: human note: the ordering did not change so now the scale is broken again)
Fixed the scale selection logic so it won’t pick decade/year for a short range (human note: I think the true cause of the bug was the reordering of the units array ;))

Added quarter-hour support in the scale logic and adjusted unit selection so it actually appears between hours and minutes (bug: it does not because the scale is broken now: only one tick labeled '2000' shows up)
Lowered the spacing thresholds for scale unit selection so minutes can appear at deeper zoom levels

Fixed the grouping layout by decoupling vertical stacking from the time scale. The line offsets are now in screen pixels (not “seconds”), so day/week/month/year grouping actually stacks into distinct horizontal lanes.

Tightened the statistics layout so it uses less vertical space by reducing padding, margins, and font sizes.

Updated the generator to include all files that are not ignored by git, regardless of tracked/untracked status. It now uses git ls-files --cached --others --exclude-standard, so untracked but not ignored files are included, and ignored files are excluded. Excluded folders/files handling stays the same.
